chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – Selling the goods and its buying

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/213)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/213)
text: لَا بَأْسَ بِأَجْرِ السِّمْسَارِ إِنَّمَا يَشْتَرِي لِلنَّاسِ يَوْماً بَعْدَ يَوْمٍ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ مُسَمًّى إِنَّمَا هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْأُجَرَاءِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Wallad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and someone else from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘There is no problem with the fee of a middle-man. But rather, he buys for the people on a day after day with something named. But rather, he is at the status of the employees’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from and Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn Mahbub from abu Wallad from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and others from abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Brokerage fee is not unlawful; he only buys for people every now and then for a known amount. He in fact works on hire.’”
